title: sparganium minimum var. rostratum
intro: Sparganium minimum var. rostratum, also known as bur-reed, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the Typhaceae family, characterized by its rostrate (narrowly pointed) leaf tips and distinctive brownish-red inflorescence. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Sparganium minimum var. rostratum include its ability to alleviate urinary tract issues, reduce swelling and pain, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat conditions such as cystitis, kidney stones, and rheumatism.
